page: Microsoft Azure D13_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX and AVX512F instruction sets. The server is equipped with 56.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 410 GB of local storage. No GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, as well as PassMark memory scores, consistently register in the average tier.

The primary tradeoff of the D13_v2 is its weak single-threaded execution speed and lower efficiency in text processing and decompression tasks, which rank in the bottom 10% to 25%. Conversely, its 56.0 GB memory capacity and average multi-core scaling support workloads such as static web serving, software compilation with Clang, and database operations, which all perform in the middle 50% tier. It is also capable of handling basic, small-scale LLM prompt processing, though text generation speeds remain weak. This profile makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads, relational databases, and multi-threaded application servers that require moderate memory density.
description: The Microsoft Azure D13_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8171M processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 56.0 GB of RAM and 410 GB of local storage, without any integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution, single-threaded compression, and text processing fall into the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently rank in the average middle 50% tier. This resource profile makes the D13_v2 suitable for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded application servers, software compilation, and moderate database operations where memory capacity is required but single-threaded processing speed is not a critical constraint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D13_v2 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with 56 GB RAM and 410 GB local storage, offering average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth alongside lower single-core efficiency.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D13_v2 features 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 56 GB RAM, and 410 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core and database performance.
tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine featuring eight dedicated cores, moderate memory density, and local storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M vCPUs operating at 2.6 GHz
56.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
410 GB of local storage capacity without integrated GPUs
Average multi-core scaling, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Weak single-core execution and single-threaded compression speeds
Suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and databases
